samples.xml_description:
<module id="RAM">
    <description>Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="write_en">Write enable signal to initiate a write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_data" width_description="[5:0]">Data to be written to the RAM.</input>
        <input id="read_en">Read enable signal to initiate a read operation.</input>
        <input id="read_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the read operation.</input>
        <output id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Output signal representing the data read from the RAM.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">6</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">8</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Define a register array, RAM, with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits. Initialize all memory locations to 000000. Implement separate always blocks for read and write operations triggered by the positive edge of the clock signal or the negative edge of the reset signal. On reset, clear all memory locations and the read_data register. During write operations, store incoming data at the specified address. During read operations, retrieve data from the specified address and update the read_data register.</implementation>
</module>
